title: [FIRRTL][CheckCombLoops] Non-deterministic ability to find a combinational cycle
body:
The following example has a combinational loop, but sometimes it is not found by the pass.  Running `firtool` on this example:
```
circuit Foo:

  module Bar:
    input a : UInt<1>
    output b : UInt<1>
    b <= a

  module Baz:
    input a : UInt<1>
    output b : UInt<1>
    output c : {d : UInt<1>}
    b <= a
    c.d <= a

  module Foo:
    inst bar of Bar
    inst baz of Baz

    bar.a <= baz.b
    baz.a <= bar.b
```
Will sometimes produce no error, or sometimes produce:
```
./test.fir:15:10: error: detected combinational cycle in a FIRRTL module, sample path: Foo.{bar.a <- baz.b <- baz.a <- bar.b <- bar.a}
  module Foo:
```

I suspect that the non-deterministic iteration order of `handlePorts` mixed with the early return might be responsible for this issue: 
https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/30a7e7a6f18dcaf598640eb432fa4264f2927ad2/lib/Dialect/FIRRTL/Transforms/CheckCombLoops.cpp#L386-L396
